narrative: OMX30 opens with a constructive backdrop: European indices rally led by CAC 40's +0.95%, NASDAQ +0.91% supports tech sentiment, and Asia shows resilience despite Shanghai weakness. The OMX30 sits +7.9% above its 200-day moving average in a confirmed bull market regime, with MACD above signal and RSI at 58.57 suggesting moderate upside without overbought extremes. However, ADX at 2.4 signals weak directional momentum—confidence should remain measured. Oil's -1.13% drop alleviates input-cost headwinds for industrials and consumer sectors, while gold's +0.96% gain provides a modest tailwind for mining. The key headwind: US 10Y yields fell 58bp, which pressures bank NIM assumptions and creates DCF compression for high-multiple tech names. Watch for mean-reversion risk if RSI pushes above 70 or breaks below 45.
morningBrief: OMX30 expected +0.4% at open on solid European momentum and broad risk appetite. CAC's +95bp outperformance and NASDAQ strength provide positive carry. Key technical: we're +210bp above the 50-MA (3123) and well-supported on the 200-MA (3085); watch 3180 as near-term resistance. ADX weakness means rallies are likely capped—use strength to trim longs. Industrials (Volvo B, Atlas Copco A) benefit from oil down and steady EUR/SEK; size accordingly. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken) face headwinds from falling yields (-58bp) offsetting NIM upside. Gold +96bp lifts Boliden outright; copper flat won't trigger secondary reactions. H&M B under pressure from EUR/SEK weakness; avoid aggressive longs. Watch 14:30 CET US Jobless Claims—a surprise beat could reignite yield rally and reverse this morning's tailwind.
drivers: Broad European strength (CAC +0.95%, DAX +0.27%) with solid correlation signal +0.606%
OMX30 structurally bullish: +7.9% above 200-MA, MACD above signal, weak ADX supports modest continuation
Commodity mixed but gold +0.96% supports defensives; oil -1.13% eases input cost pressure on industrials
USD/SEK flat (-0.37%) limits tailwind for exporters; EUR/SEK -0.30% pressure on H&M sourcing
VIX modest at 15.82; yields down -58bp supports risk appetite but limits bank NIM expansion

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil -1.13% eases input-cost pressure; EUR/SEK stability supports export pricing. No margin expansion but reduced downside risk.
US 10Y yield down 58bp compresses NIM forecasts despite potential easing cycle benefit. Near-term headwind outweighs long-term support.
NASDAQ +0.91% supports sentiment, but falling yields create DCF headwinds for growth names. Ericsson's export strength (USD/SEK flat) offers slight support.
Gold +0.96% is a direct revenue driver for Boliden; copper flat prevents secondary boost. SSAB benefits from modest industrial demand backdrop.
EUR/SEK down 30bp increases sourcing costs for H&M; oil decline offers modest offset but insufficient. EVO sentiment neutral.
Falling yields support valuations long-term but near-term uncertainty on rate trajectory. No immediate catalyst.
Risk-on backdrop limits defensive rotation benefit; no sector-specific catalyst. Monitor for any risk-off reversal.

watchList.reason: Gold +0.96% provides direct revenue uplift; copper flat avoids headwind. Best risk/reward in materials complex.
Oil down eases input costs; export-exposed with stable USD/SEK. Industrials benefit most from cost relief.
Yields down 58bp compress NIM outlook; avoid initiation into weakness.
EUR/SEK -30bp increases sourcing costs materially; no offsetting benefit in oil decline.
watchList.catalyst: FX moves, Q2 margin pressure signals

watchList.reason: NASDAQ strength is offset by falling yields creating DCF compression. Monitor sentiment but avoid momentum chasing.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish data today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key risk event—consensus expects modest tick up. Better-than-expected claims print could reignite yield rally and reverse morning tailwind.
topRisk: US data surprise (Jobless Claims) sparks yield rally, reversing morning's positive carry. Geopolitical escalation (Ukraine/Romania) could spike VIX rapidly; watch for European close if tension escalates. ADX weakness means rallies are easily faded—avoid chasing strength into resistance 3180.
technicalSummary: ADX 2.4 (very weak—reduces confidence, limit moves to expected +0.4%)
52W percentile 87% (extended from lows, but not in danger zone)
